pr.title: Mark vi0402 as matching, run ninja apply
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Marked melee/vi/vi0402.c as Matching in configure.py and committed the generated ninja apply symbol updates for GALE01. The symbol updates mainly changed several data labels from global unknown symbols to local labels or local-scoped objects, indicating the matched object now owns those literals/data references. The decomp.dev bot reported linked code increased by 768 bytes (+0.02%) and linked data by 48 bytes.
what_changed: In configure.py, changed Object(NonMatching, "melee/vi/vi0402.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/vi/vi0402.c").
In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, converted .data symbols un_80400088 and un_80400094 into local string labels @160 and @161.
Changed erase_colors_vi0601 at .sbss:0x804D6FB4 from global scope to local scope while keeping the same name, address, size, and data type.
Converted .sdata2 float symbol un_804DE058 into local label @154.
The automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed linked code at 29.84% with +768 bytes and linked data at 28.63% with +48 bytes.
smart_moves: The PR did not only flip the Matching flag; it also ran the repository apply step so symbols.txt reflected the new object ownership and local/global scope changes.
Kept generated symbol changes minimal and address-preserving: only names/scopes changed for affected data, reducing review risk.
Used local @-numbered labels for compiler-local literals/data rather than preserving misleading global un_ names.
decomp_lessons: When promoting an object from NonMatching to Matching, run the apply/update tooling and commit the resulting symbol-map changes in the same PR.
Matching an object can require symbol scope corrections even when source files are not changed in the PR slice; data previously emitted as global unknowns may become local labels.
For GALE01 symbol-map cleanup, preserve exact addresses, sizes, sections, alignment, and data annotations while changing only the evidence-backed name or scope.
A PR with no source diff can still represent meaningful decomp progress if configure.py changes an object to Matching and decomp.dev reports linked-code growth.
naming_conventions: Unknown global data symbols use address-derived names such as un_80400088, un_80400094, and un_804DE058.
Local compiler/generated labels in symbols.txt use @number names such as @154, @160, and @161.
Named project symbols such as erase_colors_vi0601 may remain named while changing scope from global to local when tooling determines local ownership.
Section and data annotations in symbols.txt follow patterns like .data, .sbss, .sdata2 plus type:object, size, scope, align, and data kind comments.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promote vi0402.c to Matching only after the linked object is accepted by the build/matching pipeline.
Use ninja apply or equivalent generated-symbol application to synchronize local symbol labels after an object starts matching.
Treat local strings and local float constants as @-numbered local symbols when matching requires them to be object-local instead of exported unknown globals.
Use the decomp.dev linked-code/data report as a sanity check for the expected progress delta after marking an object matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the PR slice.
The only comment was the decomp.dev bot report: linked code +768 bytes (+0.02%), linked data +48 bytes, and 'No changes' beyond the reported metrics.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that mark melee/vi/*.c objects as Matching.
Find PRs where ninja apply converts un_ symbols to @number local labels.
Find symbol-map changes that switch scope:global to scope:local for .sbss or .sdata2 objects.
Find PRs with decomp.dev reports showing linked code increases after configure.py Matching changes.
